slides.title: The garden may be a *longevity tool*.
slides.sub: In the world's Blue Zones, daily gardening is a quiet habit linked to longer, healthier years.

slides.title: A Blue Zones habit
slides.body: People in the longest-living regions tend gardens well into their nineties. It is *gentle, daily movement* woven into ordinary life.

slides.title: Movement that lasts
slides.body: Bending, lifting and digging are low-impact effort the body welcomes. Steady activity like this *can support* mobility and strength as you age.

slides.title: Light and calm
slides.body: Time outdoors brings sunlight your body uses to make vitamin D, and quiet hands-in-the-dirt focus that *may lower stress*.

slides.title: Friendly microbes
slides.body: Contact with garden soil exposes you to diverse microbes that *may help diversify* the gut, a system tied to long-term health.

slides.title: Small habit, real years
slides.body: Movement, sunlight, calm and soil add up. A short daily session is an *easy longevity ritual* almost anyone can keep.

slides.title: Turn small habits into *good years*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the movement, steps, sleep and food behind habits like this, all in one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The longest-living people on earth share a quiet habit: they garden. 🌿🌞

Low-impact movement, sunlight, stress relief and contact with soil microbes that may diversify the gut. Small ritual, real years. Save this. 🔖
